scenario: You raised pre-flop with A‚ô£K‚ô¶ and c-bet. Board K‚ôÝ7‚ô•2‚ô£. You have top pair top kicker.
question: Which Hardin hand-strength category is this, and what's the default plan?
options: Strong value ‚Äî bet 1-3 streets for value
Medium strength ‚Äî check down for showdown
explanation: Top pair top kicker on a dry board is a STRONG VALUE hand. Hardin's default: bet for value across multiple streets ‚Äî value betting is where most micro-stakes profit comes from. You want worse Kx, pairs, and draws to pay you off.

scenario: You raised with A‚ôÝJ‚ô£, c-bet. Board J‚ô¶8‚ôÝ5‚ô• ‚Äî but a tight player called your flop bet and the turn brings a 2. You have top pair, decent kicker.
question: Category and default line on the turn against a tight caller?
options: Medium strength ‚Äî pot control, check or thin value vs weak players only
explanation: Top pair with an okay (not top) kicker is MEDIUM STRENGTH. Hardin: usually check these down for showdown value, thin-value-bet only vs weaker opponents heads-up. Against a tight player who called your flop bet, their range is firming up ‚Äî don't bloat the pot with a one-pair hand. Pot control.

scenario: You raised with Q‚ôÝJ‚ôÝ, c-bet. Board T‚ôÝ9‚ô¶4‚ô£. You have an open-ended straight draw + backdoor flush + two overcards.
question: Category and default line?
options: Draw ‚Äî semi-bluff with good equity + fold equity + barrel potential
explanation: This is a premium DRAW: 8 outs to the straight, two overcards, backdoor flush. Hardin: semi-bluff when you have drawing equity + fold equity + barrel potential ‚Äî all three are present. You can bet the flop and credibly double-barrel many turns (any spade, K, 8, Q, J). Strong semi-bluff.

scenario: You raised with A‚ô•5‚ô•, c-bet. Board K‚ô£9‚ôÝ4‚ô¶ (you have ace-high, no pair, no draw). A calling station called your flop c-bet.
question: Category and line against a calling station?
options: Weak hand ‚Äî check/fold, don't keep firing at a station
explanation: Ace-high with no pair and no draw is a WEAK hand. Hardin: weak-hand play is situational ‚Äî c-bet bluff only vs high-fold-to-cbet opponents. Against a CALLING STATION (who never folds), firing again is lighting money on fire. Check/fold unless you improve. The player type determines the line.

scenario: YOUR HAND. You called a 3-bet with K‚ôÝT‚ôÝ. Board 6‚ô¶Q‚ô£J‚ôÝ ‚Äî you have K-high with a gutshot (any A makes Broadway). Villain (TAG 3-bettor) bets.
question: Honestly categorize K-high+gutshot vs a 3-bettor's range here.
options: Weak/marginal draw ‚Äî only 4 outs to a gutshot, no pair, vs a strong capped-up range; check/fold-ish
explanation: Your real hand. K-high with only a gutshot (4 outs) against a TAG's QQ+/AK 3-betting range is a WEAK/marginal holding, not a real draw. You have no pair, no showdown value, and minimal equity vs a range that smashed Q-J-6. This is the hand you lost ~97 BB with by continuing. Correctly categorizing it as weak = fold to the flop bet and move on.
